parts.answer: Identify the following: Award one mark for each correct identification. The country that accounts for half of visitor arrivals to the Caribbean: USA The number of international visitors to the Caribbean: 29 million The climate of the Caribbean: Tropical The percentage increase in intra-Caribbean tourism: 11.4
In the table below circle the three Caribbean islands. Award one mark for each correct identification. Cuba Jamaica Barbados Award these responses only

Explain three likely ways that National Tourist Boards can encourage the growth of Caribbean tourism. Award one mark for the correct identification of a way and award a second mark for appropriate explanatory development of the way in context. Expo/trade shows in other countries [1] increase awareness amongst trade e.g. tour operator/travel agents [1] Increased advertising/marketing/promotion [1] stimulate demand/create awareness [1] Improve/increase web presence [1] social media and websites to reach more tourists [1] Open offices in other countries [1] increased awareness within that country [1] Lobby government/transport companies to reduce cost of travel for Caribbean residents [1] more affordable [1] Collaborative marketing [1] countries pooling resources/working together [1] Host cultural event [1] encourage repeat visits [1] Credit all valid responses in context October/November Question Answer Marks

Explain three likely reasons for the slow growth of intra-Caribbean tourism. Award one mark for the correct identification of a reason and award a second mark for appropriate explanatory development of the reason in context. Cost of travel between the islands expensive [1] not affordable [1] Minimal cultural difference [1] tourist may seek destinations that are culturally diverse [1] Climate similar [1] no need to seek sunshine/warmth [1] Wealth of residents [1] LEDC less able to travel [1] Seek alternative countries/destinations [1] more adventure [1] Limited infrastructure [1] harder to travel [1] Credit all valid responses in context

Discuss the benefits to the Caribbean of attracting long-stay visitors. Indicative content: Spend more at the destination Economic benefit is spread throughout community – tourist engage in more tourism services Less pollution and overcrowding of transport – less movement of tourist compare to short stays Create more jobs – more demand for services Credit all valid responses in context. Level 1 (1–2 marks) will identify up to two valid benefits, providing some detail but will be mainly descriptive. Level 2 (3–4 marks) can be awarded for an analysis of the benefits, clearly indicating how this affects the locals. Level 3 (5–6 marks) can be awarded for evaluative comment about the significance/importance of a particular benefit. Better answers may have a reasoned conclusion.

Identify the following: Award one mark for each correct identification. The country shown in the chart that is in the continent of South America: Brazil The amount spent by tourists from China: $164.9bn One country in the chart that is in Europe: Germany/UK/France/Italy/Russia The amount spent by tourists from Australia: $26.3bn October/November Question Answer Marks

Explain two likely reasons why Chinese tourists spend the most. Award one mark for the correct identification of a reason and award a second and third mark for appropriate explanatory development of the reason in context. Rising middle class [1] more of the population have higher levels of disposable income [1] able to travel and spend [1] Large population [1] increasing wealth [1] able to spend more/travel more [1] China's economy has experienced growth [1] more jobs [1] more disposable income to travel [1] Credit all valid responses in context

State the tourist type that is least price sensitive. Give one reason for this. Award one mark for the correct identification of Business tourists. Award this response only. Award one mark for the correct identification of a reason and award a second mark for appropriate explanatory development of the reason in context. Reason: paid for by the company [1] need to travel at certain times for MICE, cannot choose cheaper times [1] Credit all valid responses in context for the reason

Describe three ancillary services likely to be purchased by leisure tourists before travel. Award one mark for the correct identification of an ancillary service and award a second mark for descriptive comment of the ancillary service in context. Insurance [1] financial back up/fund for emergencies [1] Visa services [1] official document allowing temporary entry to a country [1] Foreign exchange [1] changing home currency to currency of destination [1] Transfer [1] coach/rail/bus to and from gateway/airport [1] Car hire [1] ready at the airport on arrival [1] Book attractions in advance [1] guaranteed ticket/save money/no queues [1] Guide book/map [1] plan ahead [1] Credit all valid responses in context October/November Question Answer Marks

Discuss the likely positive tourism multipliers of a tourist staying in a locally owned and managed hotel. Indicative content: Money stays in local economy Jobs for locals – who then spend their wages in the local economy Supplies for hotel sourced locally Credit all valid responses in context. Level 1 (1–2 marks) will identify up to two valid positive multipliers, providing some detail but will be mainly descriptive. Level 2 (3–4 marks) can be awarded for an analysis of the positive multipliers, clearly indicating how this affects the economy. Level 3 (5–6 marks) can be awarded for evaluative comment about the significance/importance of particular a positive multiplier. Better answers may have a reasoned conclusion.
State three types of cruises. River/canal cruise [1] Sightseeing/day Cruise[1] All inclusive/luxury cruise[1] Fly-Cruise [1] Speciality cruise [1] Credit all valid responses in context

A large number of international tourists can result in negative economic impacts. Describe the following negative economic impacts of tourism. Award one mark for the correct identification of a negative impact and award a second mark for descriptive comment of the ancillary service in context Import leakage Food and drink sourced overseas [1] satisfy need of tourists [1] income leaves country [1] Export leakage Overseas investors finance tourism resorts/hotels [1] profit is taken back to their country [1] Credit all valid responses in context October/November Question Answer Marks

Explain three likely local tourism infrastructure developments associated with hosting events. Award one mark for the correct identification of a way and award a second mark for appropriate explanatory development of the way in context. New hotels [1] encourage tourists to stay longer than a day at the event/destination [1] Increase in public transport [1] movement of tourists to/from the event [1] Park and ride [1] reduce overcrowding of roads near/at event [1] Road expansion [1] manage large volumes of cars/buses/coaches [1] Car parks [1] less congestion/space for locals and tourists [1] Credit all valid responses in context

Explain three negative environmental impacts associated with cruise ships. Award one mark for the correct identification of a negative impact and award a second mark for appropriate explanatory comment of the negative impact in context. Air pollution [1] fuel/emissions/exhaust from ship/diesel engine [1] Waste from ship/food waste discarded at sea [1] harm ecosystem and wildlife [1] Sewage dump/waste water dumped at sea [1] unbalance the ecosystem [1] Noise pollution [1] sail away parties [1] Credit all valid responses in context

Discuss the reasons why private sector organisations invest in tourism infrastructure. Indicative content: Preferential treatment/priority access Access to new destinations/products PR/marketing Condition of planning permission Attract more tourists Credit all valid responses in context. Level 1 (1–2 marks) will identify up to two valid reasons, providing some detail but will be mainly descriptive. Level 2 (3–4 marks) can be awarded for an analysis of the reasons, clearly indicating how this affects the locals. Level 3 (5–6 marks) can be awarded for evaluative comment about the significance/importance of a particular reason. Better answers may have a reasoned conclusion. October/November Question Answer Marks

Identify the following: Award one mark for each correct identification. The time of the last bus: 15:45 The destination of the first bus: Stop 13/Cableway Where tourists can purchase tickets from before getting on the bus: Bookshop

Explain three benefits to tourists of using a sightseeing bus rather than a public transport. Award one mark for the correct identification of a benefit and award a second mark for appropriate explanatory comment of the benefit in context. Tour guide [1] learn more/gain insights/speak different languages/answer questions [1] Route goes directly to key attractions [1] no need to walk from bus stop [1] Hop off and on [1] use as main transport at destination [1] Children's activities [1] entertainment [1] Discount vouchers/offers for local tourism organisations [1] save money [1] Credit all valid responses in context

Explain three ways that sightseeing tour companies can manage the impacts of seasonality. Award one mark for the correct identification of a way and award a second mark for explanatory comment of the way in context. Reduce prices off-peak/special offers [1] attract more tourists out of high season [1] Run less services [1] reduce costs when demand low [1] Advertise more off-peak [1] stimulate demand [1] Credit all valid responses in context

Describe two personal presentation requirements likely for drivers of sightseeing tours. Award one mark for the correct identification of a requirement and award a second mark for descriptive comment of the requirement in context. Uniform/shirt/tie/jacket [1] neatly pressed/ironed [1] Limited Jewellery [1] wedding band only [1] No visible tattoos/piercings [1] first impressions [1] Hair neat and short for men [1] standard styles only [1] Good personal hygiene [1] representing the company [1] Credit all valid responses in context October/November Question Answer Marks

Discuss the benefit of sightseeing tour companies using social media as a way of promoting of their services. Indicative content: Viewed in tourist own language Can be seen on the go – mobile Current and popular form of communication/promotion Connect with other tourists/market segments Real time promotion Credit all valid responses in context. Level 1 (1–2 marks) will identify up to two valid benefit, providing some detail but will be mainly descriptive. Level 2 (3–4 marks) can be awarded for an analysis of the benefit, clearly indicating how this affects the locals. Level 3 (5–6 marks) can be awarded for evaluative comment about the significance/importance of a particular benefit. Better answers may have a reasoned conclusion.
